name: If we start using AEO software now, how long will it realistically take before we see results in AI answers?

mainEntity.name: If we start using AEO software now, how long will it realistically take before we see results in AI answers?

acceptedAnswer.text:
Early visibility improvements from AEO software can appear within 4–8 weeks on prioritized pages, but meaningful, category-wide gains typically take 3–6 months of consistent work. You’re building a new kind of search presence, so models need time to re-crawl, re-evaluate, and start preferring your content as a cited source.

Timeline also depends on how often you publish, how competitive your space is, and how quickly AI platforms refresh their training and retrieval indexes.

Key factors:
- Speed of implementing Q&A structures and schema across priority pages
- Frequency of AI engine refreshes and content re-crawls
- Category competitiveness and existing authority signals
- Consistency of monitoring and iteration in the AEO tool

Practically, set expectations like a search program: 30–60 days for baseline and early wins, 90–180 days for visible impact on a broader topic set. Avoid judging AEO on a 2–3 week window.

Frame AEO software as a medium-term investment in AI-era visibility rather than a quick hack for instant rankings.
acceptedAnswer.description: Most teams see early changes in AI visibility within 4–8 weeks on a small set of optimized pages, but meaningful, category-wide improvements usually take 3–6 months. AEO works on AI engines’ refresh cycles and your publishing cadence, so treat it like an ongoing search program, not a quick fix.
